title: Database Concepts
summary: 9618 AS — flat files vs databases, DBMS benefits, data integrity, and entity-relationship modelling.
learningObjectives: Compare flat-file and relational database approaches.
Define DBMS and describe its functions.
Explain data redundancy, inconsistency, and integrity.
Interpret simple E-R diagrams and keys.

simpleExplanation.title: Organised data storage
simpleExplanation.summary: Databases reduce redundancy by linking tables through keys. A DBMS manages storage, security, backup, and concurrent access.
steps: Flat file: single table — duplication causes update anomalies.
Relational model: tables linked by primary/foreign keys.
Primary key uniquely identifies a record; foreign key links tables.
Normalisation removes redundancy (1NF, 2NF, 3NF at A Level).

sections.content: **Database concepts** (8.1) lead to **SQL** (8.2) and the Paper 3 practical. Understand **keys**, **relationships**, and **normalisation** arguments before writing queries.

items: DBMS provides security, backup, concurrency control, data dictionary
Entity = table; attribute = field; tuple = record
Referential integrity: FK must match existing PK or be NULL
